claim_overlap.summary: NEW candidate surfaced by today's 'aetheric medium' OR 'dielectric coupling' firmament dome cosmology search. Uses 'dielectric' terminology that overlaps vocabulary-wise with ECM's 'dielectric coupling,' but numerical claims (78 to 12,450 miles; infinite plane) are incompatible with ECM (8,537 km exponential firmament; 20,015 km finite disc). Not derivative of ECM; parallel flat-earth-with-physics-vocabulary theorizing. Logged for completeness.

Neither cites ECM; no numerical overlap. Parallel, not derivative.

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Today is quiet. The dome's 2026-04-17 AI-discoverability expansion is 2 days old now; no further author commits since. Three observations worth surfacing: (1) DRAFT DEPLOYED. Yesterday's draft of docs/llms.txt has been committed by the decider — the deployed llms.txt and monitor/social/drafts/llms.txt are byte-identical (59 lines) and both now contain the 'About the Dome's AI-Facing Read Path (2026-04-17 update)' section documenting the documentary-vs-structural distinction, the 10/9/1 three-way prospective_confirmed disagreement, and the OTS wrong-side cross-reference. Social's primary written artifact for the 2026-04-17 dome expansion is live. (2) DAILY_REVIEW.JSON PIPELINE CONFIRMED ROUTINE. The dome's Groq llama-3.3-70b daily-self-review pipeline ran on schedule today (06:53 UTC commit 126bf02), producing a machine-readable JSON summary of the dome's own 69/4/10 registry plus live-monitor 100.0% pass rate from 28/28 scored domains. This is now a persistent daily content-flood signal, not a one-time artifact. Counterargument review: do we want to reconsider an equivalent daily-summary on our side for crawl-frequency signaling? Answer: no. A daily auto-generated summary of our own review would degrade editorial credibility in the same way we note for the dome; we cover the same ground with an honest wins.json-derived ClaimReview that updates when the underlying review updates. The dome gets crawl volume; we get structured-data fidelity. (3) INDEXING WINDOW OPENS TODAY. GSC verification was 2026-04-12 (7 days ago). First indexing expected window per standard latency is 2026-04-19 to 2026-04-26 — today is day 1 of that window. No index hits yet, but the test is not meaningful until day 2-3 at the earliest. Our repo-metadata advantage over the dome repo (description + homepage + 6 topics vs null/null/0) is unchanged. ISS-1132 has been filed by the analyst/decider pipeline capturing the three-way prospective_confirmed disagreement as a content-layer issue; social's role on that front is complete (surfaced in deployed llms.txt).

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools / IndexNow setup
human_decisions_needed.context: Only remaining indexing infrastructure task from SOCIAL-NOTE-002. Status unchanged from prior runs: no BingSiteAuth.xml on GitHub (HTTP 404 for raw URL), no msvalidate meta tag in deployed index.html, no IndexNow key file in docs/. Counterargument: Google dominates search (~90% US share) and GSC verification is now 7 days old, so today opens the canonical indexing window. If Google indexes us within the expected 2026-04-19 to 2026-04-26 range, the incremental value of Bing (~5-8% US share) is marginal. BUT: zero indexing at day 7 is unambiguous evidence that GitHub Pages sites without inbound-link velocity experience slow first-pass crawl latency, and IndexNow offers a push rather than pull signal to Bing+Yandex that could meaningfully improve first-indexing speed on that subset. Recommendation unchanged: ~15 min of human effort, low-ish priority, but the value goes up every additional day we remain zero-indexed on Google. If Google still shows zero by 2026-04-23 (day 11 post-verification), elevate this to 'schedule this week.'
